tasks.name: Daily standup — assess project state
tasks.prompt: Run the cabala-autonomous-loop ASSESS step (read PROGRESS.md, latest cycle-*.md, task-queue.md, git status). Write a 5-line daily standup to memory/standup-YYYY-MM-DD.md with: (1) onde o projeto está, (2) próxima fase prioritária, (3) blockers, (4) test count atual, (5) 1 ação concreta para hoje. NÃO implemente nada — só relatório.

tasks.name: Weekly instinct evolution + memory cleanup
tasks.prompt: Sunday night maintenance. Run in order: (1) `python3 ~/.claude/plugins/cache/ecc/ecc/2.0.0-rc.1/skills/continuous-learning-v2/scripts/instinct-cli.py prune` to clean obsolete instincts, (2) `instinct-cli evolve --generate` to refresh skill/command/agent catalog, (3) update MEMORY.md index with new cycle numbers, (4) review task-queue.md and move stale items to Pré-existentes if they're 2+ weeks old. Write summary to memory/maintenance-YYYY-MM-DD.md.

tasks.name: Hourly autonomous cycle (ACTIVE — multi-hour mode)
tasks.prompt: Execute ONE iteration of the cabala-autonomous-loop. Read /home/skynet/.claude/projects/-home-skynet-cabala-dos-caminhos/memory/loop-prompt.md for the full 5-step protocol (ASSESS → PLAN → EXECUTE → VERIFY → EVOLVE). Run for up to 45 minutes; if a Fase finishes, commit and update cycle memory. Be persistent: do NOT stop at minor blockers — register them in cycle memory as 'Pré-existentes' and pick the next task from the queue. Only stop if build fails 5x in a row, OR token budget at 90%, OR completely out of queued tasks.
tasks.comment: Hourly autonomous cycle — ENABLED for multi-hour operation. Cron pattern '7 * * * *' = top of every hour, :07 (avoids the :00 round-number spike). Set enabled: false to pause.

tasks.name: Quick cycle every 20 min (ACTIVE — high-throughput mode)
tasks.prompt: Quick autonomous cycle. Read /home/skynet/.claude/projects/-home-skynet-cabala-dos-caminhos/memory/loop-prompt.md (5-step protocol) and execute ONE short iteration: pick a small task from task-queue.md (≤15 min), execute, run npm run build + npm run test:run, commit if green, update cycle memory. Designed to be FAST and CHEAP — don't start anything that needs >15 min. If task-queue.md is empty, do a small refactor or doc update; if nothing, just report status and exit. This runs alongside the hourly cycle for higher throughput during the day.
tasks.comment: Quick cycle every 20 min — fills gaps between hourly cycles. Cron '13,33,53 * * * *' = :13, :33, :53 of every hour (off-round to avoid :00 spike).

tasks.name: Night shift — long-running cycle (ACTIVE — overnight mode)
tasks.prompt: NIGHT SHIFT — long autonomous session. Read /home/skynet/.claude/projects/-home-skynet-cabala-dos-caminhos/memory/loop-prompt.md and run as many iterations as possible between 23:00 and 08:00 (up to 9 hours). Each iteration follows the 5-step protocol. Pick tasks from task-queue.md systematically (P0 first, then P1, then P2). Run the full verify triad after each task. Commit after each successful iteration. Update cycle memory with progress. Be AGGRESSIVE about completing work — do not stop unless: (1) build broken for 5+ consecutive iterations, (2) token budget > 95%, (3) you have committed at least 5 Fase-level changes (then stop to rest before dawn). When done, write a night-shift-summary to memory/night-shift-YYYY-MM-DD.md with: total iterations, total commits, total test delta, and what remains in the queue.
tasks.comment: Night shift — kicks off at 23:00, designed to run for hours overnight. Cron '0 23 * * *' = 11pm every day.
